title: The Curious Little Owl
moral: Curiosity opens the door to knowledge. When you ask questions and explore, you discover wonderful things you never knew before.
moralZh: 好奇心是通向知识的钥匙。当你提出问题、勇于探索时，你会发现许多从未想象过的奇妙事物。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Deep in the Whispering Woods, there lived a little owl named Oliver. Oliver was the most curious owl in the whole forest.

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "What is beyond that hill? Why does the river sing? How do stars stay in the sky? I want to know everything!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: One evening, Oliver heard an old story from his grandmother about a hidden cave behind the Moonlit Waterfall.

paragraphs.speaker: Grandma Owl
paragraphs.text: "They say the cave has walls that glow like rainbows. But no owl has been brave enough to find it."

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "A cave that glows? I must find it! Where does the path start?"

paragraphs.speaker: Grandma Owl
paragraphs.text: "Follow the river upstream until you hear the waterfall. But remember, Oliver, never explore alone at night."

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "I promise, Grandma! I will go first thing in the morning!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The next morning, Oliver flew along the river. The sound of rushing water grew louder and louder.

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "The waterfall! Grandma was right, it is beautiful! Now, where could the cave be?"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Oliver searched carefully behind the waterfall. He found a narrow path hidden behind a curtain of sparkling water.

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "A hidden path! Let me explore it. I wonder what is inside..."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Oliver stepped through the waterfall and entered a large cave. His eyes grew wide with amazement.

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "Wow! The walls... they are covered in crystals! They glow in every color of the rainbow!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The cave was filled with ancient crystals that sparkled like stars. In the center, there was a small stone tablet with strange writing.

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "What does this say? I cannot read these ancient words. I wish I knew what they meant."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Just then, a wise old turtle named Professor Sheldon slowly walked into the cave.

paragraphs.speaker: Sheldon
paragraphs.text: "Ah, I see a curious young owl has finally discovered this place. It has been many years since anyone found it."

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "Can you read the writing on this stone tablet? What does it say?"

paragraphs.speaker: Sheldon
paragraphs.text: "It says: 'The greatest treasure is not what you find, but what you learn along the way.' These ancient words remind us that asking questions is the first step to wisdom."

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "So being curious is a good thing? I always ask so many questions..."

paragraphs.speaker: Sheldon
paragraphs.text: "It is the best thing! Every great discovery in the world started with someone asking 'Why?' and 'What if?' Never stop being curious, young one."

paragraphs.speaker: Oliver
paragraphs.text: "Thank you, Professor! I will keep exploring and asking questions. There is so much to discover!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Oliver flew home and told his grandmother all about the crystal cave. From that day on, every time he asked a question, he remembered that curiosity is the greatest gift of all.
